value.found_quote: Antigravity is architected as an agent-first platform, meaning the Agent Manager (Mission Control) automatically delegates specific tasks to specialized Sub-agents to optimize performance and context usage. For example, when a task requires web research or documentation lookup, the system invokes the Browser Subagent; for command-line operations, it uses the Terminal Subagent.
value.rationale: The page (a Google AI Developers Forum thread) describes that Antigravity's Agent Manager / Mission Control automatically delegates tasks to specialized built-in Sub-agents (e.g., Browser Subagent, Terminal Subagent), with the main model focusing on high-level reasoning while specialized models handle execution in parallel. This substantiates that Google's Antigravity offers subagents. Note: the page distinguishes these built-in/automatic subagents from user-creatable custom subagents (which users are requesting as a future feature), but the named offering — Antigravity subagents — is described as currently existing. The claim's specific framing (AgentKit 2.0, 16 specialized agents) is not on the page, but the core offering of Antigravity subagents is substantiated.
